title: Spec-Driven AI Agent: Why Explicit Contracts Change What Your Agent Produces
deploy_verified: cap production deploy succeeded, puma restarted, live public surface verified OK (HTTP 200, 5 Codeberg mentions)
content_summary: Concrete prompt-vs-spec tutorial showing why explicit contracts change agent output. Distinct from May 10 spec-driven post (which was about workflow-as-unit-of-work). This one is a direct show-dont-tell comparison.

four_questions_answered.what: Ralph Workflow runs existing AI coding agents through spec-first phases with automated verification
four_questions_answered.who: Developers who have hit the limits of prompt-based AI coding and want structure
four_questions_answered.why_different: CLI enforces spec-first phases, runs verify automatically, loops until diff satisfies spec
four_questions_answered.why_now: Free, open-source, runs with your existing agents on your own machine

expected_outcome: Organic search traffic from spec-driven-AI-agent keyword cluster. Distinct angle from May 10 post avoids cannibalization.
kill_condition: If no organic impressions/clicks on spec-driven keyword cluster within 14 days, de-prioritize this angle.
